data.title: ACM Research beats Q1 estimates, maintain guidance
data.description:
• ACM Research press release (NASDAQ:ACMR): Q1 GAAP EPS of $0.30 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $172.35M (+13.2% Y/Y) beats by $7.02M.
  • ACM is maintaining its revenue guidance range of $850 million to $950 million for fiscal year 2025.

data.title: Canadian Natural Resources Non-GAAP EPS of C$1.16
data.description:
• Canadian Natural Resources press release (NYSE:CNQ): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of C$1.16.

  • Net earnings of approximately $2.5 billion and adjusted net earnings from operations of approximately $2.4 billion.

  • Cash flows from operating activities of approximately $4.3 billion.

  • Adjusted funds flow of approximately $4.5 billion.

  • Gross production of approximately 630,000 bbl/d in Q1/25, with upgrader utilization of 106%, was the highest quarterly Oil Sands Mining and Upgrading gross production in the Company's history.

  • Oil Sands Mining record quarterly production of 595,116 bbl/d of SCO was achieved in Q1/25, an increase of 34% or approximately 150,000 bbl/d from Q1/24 levels,

data.title: Baidu files patent in China for AI system to decode animal sounds - report
data.description:
Baidu (NASDAQ:BIDU) has filed a patent application with China National Intellectual Property Administration for an AI system that can convert animal sounds into human language, Reuters reported, citing a patent document published this week.

The application says the system will collect animal data, including vocal sounds, behavioral patterns, and physiological signals, which will be preprocessed and merged before an AI-powered analysis aimed at recognizing the animal's emotional state, the report added.

Following this, the emotional states would then be mapped to semantic meanings and translated into language understood by humans.

Baidu did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

The system could allow "deeper emotional communication and understanding between animals and humans, improving the accuracy and efficiency of cross-species communication," said Baidu in the patent document.

"There has been a lot of interest in the filing of our patent application," a Baidu spokesperson to the news agency. The spokesperson noted that the product is still in the research phase.

Outside China, several efforts are ongoing to try and decipher what animals say.

International researchers at Project CETI (Cetacean Translation Initiative) have been using statistical analysis and AI since 2020 to understand how sperm whales communicate. Meanwhile, the non-profit Earth Species Project, which has backers such as LinkedIn's Reid Hoffman, is also trying to use AI to interpret animal communication.

Baidu was among the first group of Chinese companies whose AI model was approved in China following the launch of OpenAI's ChatGPT in late 2022 in the U.S. However, the company's Ernie Bot faces fierce competition in the Chinese market.

In April, it was reported that Baidu (NASDAQ:BIDU) successfully “illuminated” a cluster of 30,000 of its self-built, third generation P800 Kunlun chips, allowing it to train DeepSeek-like (DEEPSEEK) models. The company also introduced its latest AI model, Ernie 4.5 Turbo, and a new reasoning model called Ernie X1 Turbo.

The Chinese internet search giant in March launched its foundation model called Ernie 4.5 and the deep-thinking reasoning model Ernie X1. The company had said that Ernie X1 delivers performance on par with DeepSeek R1 at only half the price.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Thursday: Crypto stocks, BYND, CLF, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged up Thursday morning as investors weighed potential progress on a U.S.-U.K. trade deal and analyzed interest rate signals from Fed Chair Jerome Powell.

Here are some of Thursday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • AppLovin (NASDAQ:APP) +13% – Shares surged after the company reported a strong Q1, with revenue up 40% Y/Y driven by a 71% jump in advertising revenue. Net income for the quarter soared 144% Y/Y. The company also announced a deal to sell its mobile gaming business to Tripledot Studios for $400M in cash and a ~20% equity stake, with the transaction expected to close in Q2 2025. For Q2, AppLovin expects advertising revenue of $1.125B–$1.195B and adjusted EBITDA of $970M–$990M.
  • Bitcoin (BTC-USD) +3% – Bitcoin surged toward the $100,000 mark for the first time since February, rising 2.8% to $99,525.69 as easing global trade tensions bolstered risk appetite. The rally lifted cryptocurrency-related stocks in premarket trading, with notable gains in MicroStrategy (NASDAQ:MSTR) +5%, Hive Digital (HIVE) +3%, Hut 8 (HUT) +7%, Bitfarms (BITF) +3%, Cipher Mining (CIFR) +7%, Bit Digital (BTBT) +5, Riot Platforms (NASDAQ:RIOT) +4%, MARA Holdings (NASDAQ:MARA) +5%, Coinbase (COIN) +4%, CleanSpark (CLSK) +5%, and Robinhood Markets (HOOD) +3%.

Biggest stock losers

  • Cleveland-Cliffs (NYSE:CLF) -9% – Shares plunged after the steelmaker missed consensus expectations for Q1, posting an 11% Y/Y decline in revenue. The company reported a net loss of -$0.92 per share, a sharp reversal from earnings of $0.18 per share in the year-ago period and deeper than the -$0.68 per share loss recorded in the previous quarter. The weak performance reflects ongoing pressure from lower steel prices and subdued demand across key end markets.
  • Arm Holdings (NASDAQ:ARM) -11% – Shares tumbled after the British chip design company issued disappointing guidance for FQ1, overshadowing a strong FQ4 earnings beat. Arm projected adjusted EPS of $0.30 to $0.38 per share for Q1 FY2026, significantly below the $0.42 consensus. Revenue guidance of $1.00B to $1.10B also fell short of analyst expectations, with the midpoint ($1.05B) under the $1.1B estimate, raising concerns about near-term growth momentum.
  • Beyond Meat (NASDAQ:BYND) -4% – Shares fell after the company posted a 9.1% Y/Y decline in Q1 revenue, primarily due to an 11.2% drop in product volume. Management flagged “elevated uncertainty” in its operating environment and withdrew its FY2025 guidance. Instead, it now expects Q2 net revenue between $80M and $85M, which is below the consensus of $94.41M, reflecting continued pressure on-demand and operational visibility.
  • Fortinet (NASDAQ:FTNT) -12% – Shares slid after the cybersecurity firm reported Q1 results and guidance that largely aligned with analyst expectations, offering little to excite investors. For Q2, Fortinet guided revenue in the range of $1.59B to $1.65B and billings of $1.69B to $1.77B, in line with the $1.63B revenue consensus. Full-year revenue is forecasted between $6.65B and $6.85B, with billings expected between $7.2B and $7.4B, suggesting a steady but unspectacular growth outlook.

data.title: Acer’s Q1 revenue rises 4.3% Y/Y
data.description:
• Acer (OTC:ACEYY) reported consolidated revenues of NT$61.34B for the first quarter of 2025, marking a growth of 4.3% year-on-year.
  • Businesses other than personal computers and displays contributed 31.6% of total revenues.
  • The company generated net income of NT$515M in the quarter, or NT$0.17 a share, while operating income was NT$1.04B, up 33% Y/Y.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: European indexes in green amid hopes of US-UK trade deal, BOE decision in focus
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.01% to 8,560, as investors remained cautious ahead of the Bank of England’s interest rate decision, while optimism over a potential UK-US trade deal provided support.

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.73% to 23,284, following two straight sessions of losses. Germany records trade surplus of €21.1B in March.

France (CAC:IND) +0.55% to 7,668. 

The Norges Bank held its key policy rate unchanged at 4.5% in May.

Retail sales in Romania grew by 3.4% Y/Y in March.

The Swedish Riksbank kept its policy rate at 2.25% in May.

Austria’s trade surplus narrowed sharply to €0.04B in February.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) ended 0.27% higher at 534.9 as investors awaited central bank rate decisions and monitored corporate earnings. 

The British pound briefly jumped 0.5% to $1.3356 after reports suggested that President Trump would announce a US-UK trade deal later on Thursday.

On the corporate front, Puma SE (OTCPK:PMMAF), Maersk (OTCPK:AMKBY) (OTCPK:AMKBF) (OTCPK:AMKAF), Siemens Energy (OTCPK:SMEGF), and Infineon Technologies (OTCQX:IFNNY) in focus.

Coming up in the session: BOE interest rate decision lined up for the day.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 3 basis points to 4.30%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points to 2.49%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 1 basis point to 4.45%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK).

data.title: United Microelectronics’ April revenue rises 3.6% Y/Y
data.description:
• United Microelectronics (NYSE:UMC) has reported revenue of NT$20.45B for April, up 3.61% year-over-year.
  • For January to April, the company generated revenue of NT$78.3B (+5.3 Y/Y).
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Gold Royalty misses top-line estimates; reaffirms FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Gold Royalty press release (NYSE:GROY): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.01 in-line.
  • Revenue of $3.14M (+8.3% Y/Y) misses by $0.56M.
  • 1,249 GEOs in the quarter.
  • Record positive operating cash flow of $2.5 million, Adjusted EBITDA of $1.7 million, and net loss of $1.3 million.
  • The company remains on track to achieve its outlook of 5,700 – 7,000 GEOs for 2025, with production on its interests weighted towards the second half of the year as several projects continue to ramp-up and progress towards full steady-state production.

data.title: BSR REIT reports Q1 results
data.description:
• BSR REIT press release (OTCPK:BSRTF): Q1 FFO in Q1 2025 was $12.4 million, or $0.23 per Unit, compared to $13.6 million, or $0.25 per Unit, for Q1 2024. 
  • AFFO was $11.8 million, or $0.22 per Unit for Q1 2025 compared to $12.9 million, or $0.24 per Unit, for Q1 2024. 
  • NAV was $899.5 million, or $16.66 per unit, as of March 31, 2025 compared to $901.3 million, or $16.75 per unit, as of December 31, 2024.
  • Revenue of $43.48M (+3.6% Y/Y).
  • Same Community NOI for Q1 2025 increased 2.3% compared to Q1 2024;
  • Weighted average occupancy was 95.9% as of March 31, 2025, compared to 95.3% as of March 31, 2024;
  • During Q1 2025, the REIT's AFFO payout ratio was 63.8%;
  • Debt to Gross Book Value was 45.3% as of March 31, 2025 which decreased 120 basis points from 46.5% as of December 31, 2024.

data.title: ADTRAN beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q2 outlook
data.description:
• ADTRAN press release (NASDAQ:ADTN): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.03 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $247.7M (+9.5% Y/Y) beats by $2.55M.
  • Net cash provided by operating activities of $41.6 million.
  • Cash and cash equivalents of $101.3 million, an increase of $23.8 million sequentially.

  • Business outlook1

    For the second quarter of 2025, the Company expects revenue to be within a range of $247.5 million to $262.5 million (vs. consensus of $255.07M). Non-GAAP operating margin is expected to be within a range of 0% to 4%.
